status: Crafting is LIVE in Alpha 4.8.2. The core loop — Item Fabricators, blueprints, the 1–1000 material-quality system and ship-component crafting — is confirmed in-game. BUT the economy layer that would make crafting profitable (full player marketplace, auctions, offline offers) is NOT live yet, so today crafting is for self-use and gear optimization, not reliable income. No public 4.9 PTU branch is open and no major crafting overhaul is announced for it — treat any 4.9 crafting claim as unconfirmed.
overview: Crafting lets you build your own gear and ship parts instead of buying or looting them. The loop: learn a blueprint → gather raw materials → feed both into an Item Fabricator → wait out a craft timer → collect. The twist that makes SC crafting special is MATERIAL QUALITY: every material carries a quality score (1–1000), and higher quality directly raises the stats of what you craft. A crafted item can beat anything sold at a shop, because vendor stock is stuck at quality 0. Crafting is now the main way to get top-tier and military/stealth variants of weapons, armor and — since 4.8 — ship components (shields, power plants, quantum drives, coolers). It's confusing right now because drop rates, best routes and the trading economy are still half-built and mostly community-documented.

steps.title: Get an Item Fabricator
steps.detail: Buy one from shops in landing zones/stations (~95,000 aUEC, community figure). Fixed and cargo-mobile variants exist; mobile ones deploy in your hangar or ship. Stow it when done — units left deployed can vanish.

steps.title: Acquire blueprints
steps.detail: You start with a default set. New blueprints come from mission rewards, faction/guild reputation unlocks, looted crates and some vendors. Free-roaming does NOT unlock them — you must run content. Once earned, a blueprint is permanent and survives death and wipes.

steps.title: Gather raw materials
steps.detail: Mine ore (Prospector/MOLE or hand-mining), salvage hulls for RMC/Construction Materials, or dismantle looted gear at the fabricator. Aim for quality 700+ for serious gear; 500–700 for daily-wear; below 500 sell or dismantle.

steps.title: Refine if needed
steps.detail: Many recipes want refined materials, not raw ore. Refine at refinery terminals via work orders (e.g. iron ore → refined iron + carbon byproduct). The PRIMARY input quality sets the output quality — quality can't be cheaply upgraded, by design.

steps.title: Select blueprint & assign materials
steps.detail: In the fabricator UI pick a blueprint and assign materials. Hand-pick high-quality materials to push stats, or use Autofill, and watch the live stat preview. Known bug: the UI can 'ghost' stats from a previous job — hit Clear Queue before a new craft.

steps.title: Run the craft queue
steps.detail: Crafting takes minutes to hours by item size/quality. You must stay connected — there is no offline crafting in 4.8.x and disconnects can cancel jobs. Queue multiple jobs; dismantling runs on a separate queue so you can craft and break down at once.

steps.title: Collect & use (selling is limited)
steps.detail: Finished items go to your inventory. Direct online player-to-player trading exists (with recipient confirmation), but the full marketplace/auction/offline-offer system is NOT live — you can't reliably sell crafted goods yet. Craft to equip yourself, not to get rich.

qualityNote: Quality runs 1–1000, collapsed into 8 static bands in 4.8 (e.g. 700–799 → 710, 999–1000 → 1000). Higher quality directly raises crafted-item stats; vendor stock sits at quality 0, which is why crafted gear can exceed anything bought. Refining keeps the primary input's quality — you can't cheaply upgrade it.

qualityTiers.label: Premium
qualityTiers.advice: Keep for primary gear, ship components and military/stealth variants.

qualityTiers.label: Standard
qualityTiers.advice: Fine for daily-wear gear and most crafts. NPC scrap is a flat 500.

qualityTiers.label: Low
qualityTiers.advice: Sell or dismantle — only worth using for ammo/consumables.

materials.name: Mined ores (Quantanium, Taranite, Laranite, Agricium, Bexalite, Titanium, Copper, Iron, Borase, Aslarite)
materials.source: Vehicle mining (Prospector/MOLE) on belts & moons; some hand-mining
materials.note: Community spots: Quantanium/Laranite in Yela belt; Agricium/Laranite/Bexalite on Daymar. Treat exact locations as community-estimate.

materials.name: Gemstones (Hadanite, Dolivine, Aphorite)
materials.note: Hadanite reported dense on Aberdeen; exact yields not officially published.

materials.name: Refined materials (e.g. refined Iron + Carbon byproduct)
materials.note: Primary input quality sets output quality; secondary quality affects required volume.

materials.name: RMC — Recycled Material Composite
materials.note: A primary crafting input alongside ore.

materials.name: Dismantled-gear materials
materials.source: Dismantle looted FPS gear at an Item Fabricator
materials.note: NPC gear yields ~quality 500 flat; elite/boss NPCs yield higher. Cheap source for low-tier crafts and consumables.

materials.name: Commodity / bought materials
materials.note: For bulk supply. Full player-to-player material exchange isn't live yet.

materials.name: Material quality score (1–1000)
materials.source: Property of every material; rolled on mining, flat 500 for dismantled scrap
materials.note: 8 static bands since 4.8. The single most important crafting stat.

materials.name: Vanduul-origin materials
materials.note: Reported exclusive to Vanduul loot, unavailable from vendors — keeps combat relevant to crafting. Not officially confirmed.

materials.name: Farmed / organic materials
materials.note: Still in design as of 2026 — no live organic material sources confirmed.

blueprints.name: Default starter blueprints
blueprints.note: Use these to test recipes before spending rare materials.

blueprints.name: Mission-reward blueprints
blueprints.note: Primary path. Community-reported examples (community-estimate): Foxwell-type missions for shield/power & CF repeater blueprints; Covalex for transport; salvage chains for components. Exact drops/odds are NOT officially published.

blueprints.name: Reputation / faction-vendor unlocks
blueprints.howToGet: Rank up faction or guild reputation, then unlock/buy
blueprints.note: Higher rep opens restricted vendors and exclusive blueprint catalogs.

blueprints.name: Loot-crate blueprints
blueprints.howToGet: Loot crates at outposts, bunkers, derelicts, caves, UGFs, extraction sites

blueprints.name: Government Prototype / Military blueprints
blueprints.note: A distinct high tier; exact unlock conditions not publicly detailed.

blueprints.name: Higher-tier variants via Research Databank
blueprints.howToGet: Research-upgrade a known blueprint (time + resources + aUEC)
blueprints.note: Unlocks better-stat variants while keeping the cheaper version. Tier raises stats independent of material quality.

blueprints.name: Blueprint 'discovery' (decryption/scan/analysis)
blueprints.note: Sources mention blueprints being 'discovered' but the mechanic is undocumented. Salvage does NOT directly yield blueprints.

recipes.name: Mid-tier armor set (helmet / torso / plates)
recipes.needs: Armor blueprint + 500+ quality materials (700+ for top stats)
recipes.worthIt: Best beginner target — strong self-use value, beats vendor gear.

recipes.name: FPS weapons (rifles / SMGs / shotguns / snipers / pistols)
recipes.makes: Weapons that match or beat looted variants at high quality
recipes.worthIt: Worth it for military/stealth variants you can't buy. Test cheap first.

recipes.name: Ship components (shields, power plants, quantum drives, coolers, radars, ship weapons)
recipes.makes: Gameplay-affecting ship parts, often superior or exclusive vs vendor stock
recipes.needs: Component blueprint + quality materials (quality scales performance)
recipes.worthIt: Highest-value category for min-maxers — components are increasingly craft-gated.

recipes.name: Weapon attachments (scopes, grips, suppressors)
recipes.makes: Attachments above looted versions at high quality
recipes.worthIt: Situational — use cheap materials, not rare ore.

recipes.name: Consumables (MedPens, OxyPens, ammo)
recipes.needs: Low-quality materials (~300 is enough for ammo)
recipes.worthIt: Cheap from dismantled gear; self-supply only, not a money-maker. A dedicated Medical/Food Fabricator pipeline is still in development.

recipes.name: Super-heavy armor & heavy weapons (e.g. Vendetta HMG)

tips: Test new blueprints with cheap stock materials first — early experimental crafts waste rare ore.
Triage by quality: 700+ keep for primary gear, 500–700 for daily-wear, below 500 sell or dismantle.
Don't waste premium ore (high-grade Bexalite/Laranite) on accessories like magazines or attachments.
Hit 'Clear Queue' before a new craft to avoid the UI bug where stats 'ghost' from the previous job.
Stay connected during crafts — no offline crafting in 4.8.x, and disconnects can cancel jobs.
Stow your fabricator when finished; units left deployed can vanish.
Save your ship loadout after installing crafted components.
Crafting and dismantling use separate queues — break down loot while you craft.
Use Autofill to auto-assign materials, but hand-pick when you want to push a specific stat.
The strongest crafters are the most organized, not the richest — efficient gathering, storage and triage beat raw income.
roadmap: Medical / Food Fabricator pipeline — in development
Super-heavy armor & heavy weapons (Vendetta HMG etc.) — PTU/4.9
Full player marketplace, auctions & offline offers — planned (this is what would make crafting genuinely profitable)
Asynchronous / offline crafting, crafting skill trees, vehicle crafting, failure/tax mechanics — PTU/planned/unclear
uncertainty: SC crafting is new and actively evolving — much of what circulates online is community-derived, not official. SOLID (confirmed-live in 4.8.2): the core loop, Item Fabricators, the 1–1000 / 8-band quality system, sourcing via mining/salvage/refining/dismantling, ship-component crafting, blueprint persistence through wipes, no-offline-crafting and separate-dismantle-queue rules. SHAKY (community-estimate): exact blueprint drop sources and odds, best mining/farming routes and yields, the ~95k fabricator price, and the Vanduul-exclusive-material claim. NOT a money-maker yet: the full marketplace isn't live, so crafting is for self-use and optimization, not reliable income. UNCONFIRMED for 4.9: no public PTU is open and no major crafting overhaul is announced — medical/food fabricators, super-heavy armor, async crafting and skill trees are PTU/planned/unclear.
sources: Star Citizen Wiki — Crafting / Item_fabricator / Blueprint / Ship_components (starcitizen.tools)
sc-craft.tools — community blueprint database (1000+ blueprints, datamined)
ggwtb.com / mmopixel.com / theimpound.com — 4.8 crafting & blueprint guides
